---
title: "Quarto Test Document"
author: "Test Author"
format: html
execute:
  echo: true
---

# Introduction

This is the introduction of a Quarto document. The YAML front matter above and the code chunks below should be stripped — only this prose should appear.

## Analysis

Here is some analytical content that should render.

```{python}
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np

df = pd.DataFrame({'x': range(10), 'y': np.random.randn(10)})
print(df.head())
```

The paragraph above the code chunk and this paragraph should both be visible.

## Results

The results show a clear improvement over the baseline.

```{r echo=FALSE}
model <- lm(y ~ x, data = df)
summary(model)
```

## Conclusion

We conclude that the preprocessing works correctly when only this prose section is rendered and no YAML or code chunks appear.

Regular markdown still works:

- Item one
- Item two
- Item three

And **bold**, *italic*, and `code` all render fine.
